blurb_en: The Financial Times reported July 10 that OpenAI and Google have confirmed supplying AI services to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent — all three of which appear on the Pentagon's 1260H list for alleged military ties to China. The transactions are technically legal: current U.S. rules don't prohibit Chinese-owned entities from accessing frontier AI through compliant foreign jurisdictions. OpenAI suspended API access for Alibaba-affiliated accounts after detecting suspected distillation — using a frontier model's outputs to train a competing one — and notified the U.S. government. The same week, Anthropic's earlier Senate testimony resurfaced: in June, it had alleged that Alibaba ran 25,000 fraudulent accounts through Claude for 28.8 million interactions, the largest known industrial-scale AI capability theft against a U.S. lab. Washington is pushing to extend chip-style export controls to AI models; both companies are discovering that you cannot confiscate a cloud service at the border.
blurb_zh: 《金融时报》7月10日报道：OpenAI 和谷歌确认，已向在新加坡注册的阿里巴巴、百度、腾讯关联公司提供 AI 服务，而这三家企业的母公司均列于美国国防部1260H军事关联名单。目前这些交易不违法：现行出口管制规定不禁止中资企业通过第三国使用美国 AI 模型。OpenAI 发现阿里关联账户涉嫌蒸馏攻击（用其模型输出训练竞品）后立即暂停访问权限并向政府报告。与此同时，Anthropic 6月向国会提交的证词也再次浮出水面：阿里巴巴曾用约2.5万个伪造账户对 Claude 发起2880万次交互以提取 Qwen 能力，被称为迄今最大规模的 AI 能力窃取行动。美国正讨论是否将芯片式出口管制扩展到 AI 模型——两家公司都在发现，云端服务没有海关可扣押。
verification_notes: Story originally reported by Financial Times, July 10, 2026. Confirmed across Benzinga, Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, YourNews, TechStory, Blockonomi, MoneyCheck, and multiple other secondary outlets. OpenAI spokesperson confirmation of Singapore access and Alibaba distillation suspension confirmed in multiple reports. Anthropic June Senate testimony (25,000 fake accounts, 28.8M Claude interactions) confirmed via Forbes (June 26), Tom's Hardware, TechTimes (June 25). All key facts cross-referenced across at least two independent sources.

blurb_en: China's securities regulator approved Unitree Technology's IPO on July 3, making it the first humanoid-robot-focused company to list on China's A-share market. The 4.2 billion yuan ($619 million) STAR Market offering cleared review in 73 days — a record pace for that exchange — after Unitree shipped 5,500 humanoid robots globally in 2025, more than any other manufacturer. The company's G1 humanoid has dropped to a base price of $13,500; the IPO anchors the company's valuation at roughly 42 billion yuan ($6.2 billion). It is the second Chinese humanoid robot company to go public in as many months, after EngineAI's Hong Kong debut in June, and represents a moment when embodied AI has become a tradeable, benchmarkable asset class on two separate exchanges.
blurb_zh: 中国证监会7月3日批准宇树科技 IPO 申请，使其成为 A 股首家以人形机器人为主业的上市公司。拟募资约42亿元（6.19亿美元）的科创板申请从受理到过会仅用73天，创科创板最短审核纪录。宇树2025年全球人形机器人出货5500台，排名第一；G1 基础版售价已降至约9.8万元（1.35万美元）。按最低10%公开流通比例计算，估值约420亿元。继6月EngineAI赴港上市之后，两个月内中国已有两家人形机器人公司相继登陆资本市场——具身智能正式成为两个交易所上可定价、可交易的资产类别。
verification_notes: CSRC IPO registration approval July 3, 2026, confirmed by Caixin Global (primary source), Bloomberg (BigGo Finance), CGTN, EqualOcean, Global Times, Gasgoo, Futunn. 73-day review timeline confirmed across multiple sources. $619M ($4.2B yuan) figure confirmed. 5,500 units shipped in 2025 confirmed. G1 $13,500 base price confirmed. 42B yuan valuation confirmed by multiple financial outlets.

blurb_en: Tesla launched fully driverless Robotaxi service in Miami-Dade on July 3, skipping the safety-monitor transition phase it ran in every previous city — Austin, Houston, Dallas, and Phoenix all started with a person in the passenger seat; Miami never had one. The service runs on Tesla's camera-only Full Self-Driving system with no lidar and no pre-mapped road network, in a city the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has specifically flagged: an ongoing engineering probe found FSD fails to detect hazards under degraded visibility conditions such as glare and airborne obscurants. Miami's tropical downpours, intense sun glare, and high humidity are precisely the conditions on that list. The service covers roughly 10 to 14 square miles of western Miami-Dade, carefully avoiding downtown — which is either a sign of system confidence or a very honest assessment of where the edge cases live.
blurb_zh: 特斯拉7月3日在迈阿密-戴德县上线全无人 Robotaxi，成为其五座运营城市中首个从第一天起就不配备任何车内人员的城市——此前在奥斯汀、休斯顿、达拉斯和凤凰城，每辆车至少有一名坐在副驾驶的安全监控员；迈阿密从来没有。该服务采用纯摄像头 FSD 系统，无激光雷达、无预制地图，而美国公路交通安全管理局正就 FSD 在眩光、气溶胶等低能见度条件下未能有效检测危险展开工程分析调查。迈阿密的骤雨、强烈阳光和高湿度，恰好是被点名的那类条件。服务区域约10至14平方英里，覆盖迈阿密-戴德县西部，绕开了市中心——这要么说明系统有把握，要么说明开发团队非常清楚边缘情况在哪里。
verification_notes: Tesla Robotaxi Miami launch July 3, 2026, confirmed by TechTimes (July 4), TheNextWeb, notateslaapp.com, hngn.com (July 6), NewsNation, FourWeekMBA. No safety monitor from day one confirmed across all sources. NHTSA engineering analysis probe (escalated March 2026) confirmed by TechTimes. Camera-only FSD with no lidar confirmed. 10-14 square mile service area in western Miami-Dade confirmed. All key facts cross-referenced across at least two independent sources.
